summary: Iteration 16 — Smart Spend Inference (GPT-4o categorisation + privacy-safe persistence) verified end-to-end. Backend pytest: 15/15 PASS (10 new + 5 regression). Health check 14/14 HEALTHY. Frontend: login → PIN setup → skip tour → Profile → Savings Assistant → Smart Spend CTA → paste SMS dump (10 txns + OTP noise) → GPT-4o returned in ~6s → breakdown sorted desc (₹17,893 Online Shopping → ₹11,571 Fuel → Groceries → Entertainment → Food Delivery → Travel) → total ₹35,772/mo → recommend online_shopping → Apply auto-fills slider to ₹18,000 and highlights online_shopping chip → fresh-session reload auto-restores profile via GET /api/spend/profile and CTA flips to 'Refresh spend profile · Last analyzed 10 txns · ₹35,772/mo'. Privacy invariants validated: GET /api/spend/profile response contains aggregated categories/totals only — no sms_text/raw_sms/transactions field.
critical_code_review_comments: spend_intel.py _strip_json_fences + json.loads + regex fallback (lines 102-109): solid defence against GPT-4o markdown-fenced or chatty responses. 502 raised only if no JSON brace at all — appropriate.

spend_intel.py _aggregate_monthly: window_days_observed clamped to >=1 prevents divide-by-zero; monthly scaling (30/window_days) is correct. recommend_category excludes 'other' (line 142-143) so rent/utilities never get pitched as a card opportunity — UX-correct.

spend_intel.py persistence (lines 168-180): upsert keyed by user_pin, $set spreads aggregated fields only — raw SMS never written. Try/except wraps persist so user still sees inferred result if Mongo write fails. Verified empirically: spend_profiles doc has no sms_text/transactions field.
GET /spend/profile excludes _id (line 191) — JSON serialisable. Returns {exists:false} for missing rather than 404, which the frontend prefers for graceful auto-load.

CardOptimizerScreen.jsx applyInferred (lines 43-55): clamps spend to 2K-100K slider range and rounds to nearest 1K — handles edge cases where SMS reports tiny or huge monthly spend. Verified: ₹17,893 → slider 18,000.
SmartSpendSheet.jsx infer btn disabled until text.length >= 20 (line 108). Catches paste-too-small and toasts 'Paste at least one transaction SMS'. Good UX.
Privacy assurance copy ('Privacy first: we never store the raw SMS') and the green ShieldCheck block reinforce trust before the user pastes — matches the privacy-first product narrative.
api.js Spend.profile uses .catch(() => ({exists:false})) (line 122) — defensive on network/CORS failures so a flaky /api/spend/profile call never breaks the Savings Assistant load.
seed_data_creation: Test created 3 spend_profiles docs (one auto-cleaned in fixture teardown, plus per-test PIN 'DEL****' deleted in test_delete_profile, plus one persisted via UI under PIN 1234 for the test user). Safe to leave; aggregated data only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Smart Spend Inference endpoints + UI flow PASS. The GPT-4o call latency was 6-12s in this run — fine for production but tests use 60s timeout. Persistence is upsert-by-user_pin and verified privacy-safe via direct GET assertion that no sms_text/raw_sms/transactions keys leak. Iter15 regression (current-card-select, already-optimal celebration, explain modal with vs-current-block, /api/cards/click full payload) is fully covered in iteration_15.json and test_iter15_userfirst.py — was spot-checked here (dropdown filtering + baseline render). The 'bpcl-sbi-octane' card-id guess in my last script failed because that's not the real id; iter15 already verified BPCL+fuel already-optimal scenario, so no retest needed.

validations_performed.Persistence persist=true + user_pin upse: PASS (GET returns exists:true with aggregated fields, NO raw sms_text/raw_sms/transactions)
